name: Unstable_Mech

infobox.Systems: Unstable Cannon(Brute)EffectPowerful projectile that causes damage to shooter as well as target.(1 self, 2 damage)Upgrade I+1 Damage Each ()Increases damage to self and target by 1.Upgrade II+1 Damage ()Increases target damage by 1.

sections.Team: The mech is available as part of theHazardous Mechssquad.
sections.Strategy:
The Unstable Mechs weapon will self damage it with every use, and will additionally knock it backwards one tile. If using it outside of the Hazardous Mechs in aCustom Squad, it is recommended to pair it with Pilots such asMafanorAbe Isamu, or Mechs with destruction prevention equipment such as theDefense MechorSmog Mech. Note that the self damage is also treated as an attack at the Mech's current tile; thus, it will ignite forest tiles or break ice and cracked tiles that the Mech is currently occupying. The only way to prevent this is if the Unstable Mech is shielded; in which case, the shield will absorb the self-damage to the Mech and tile.
The knockback from Unstable Cannon can be useful for getting additional bump damage on enemies behind it, as long as the self-damage and bump damage are mitigated. It can be particularly useful at dealing withBlobberenemies and theirBlobsin a single action, using the projectile to hit the Blobber and the knockback damage to destroy the Blob. Additionally, the knockback can be used to fire from unsafe tiles, such as those projecting aLightning Strike, as the Unstable Cannon will push it back and away from that tile.
Because of its low default movement and knockback effect from Unstable Cannon, it's wise to invest on additional movement for the Unstable Mech first. Otherwise the knockback moving the Mech backwards has a tendency to leave it further back away from enemy spawns. Pairing it with mobile pilots such asLily Reedor letting it deploy up close withGanacan also help it alleviate this issue.
